name: dialectic-of-enlightenment
description: Max Horkheimer and Theodor Adorno's 'Dialectic of Enlightenment: Philosophical Fragments' — a foundational text of critical theory. Written in exile during WWII, the book argues that the Enlightenment's promise of human emancipation through reason reversed into new forms of domination. Key concepts: the culture industry, instrumental reason, the dialectic of myth and enlightenment, and anti-Semitism.
